title: Discrete Mathematics - Set Theory and Algebra - Relations

questions.question:
If she is my friend and you are her friend, then we are friends. Given this, the friend relationship in this context is ____________.

(i) commutative (ii) transitive (iii) implicative (iv) equivalence

(A) (i) and (ii)

(B) (iii)

(C) (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv)

(D) None of these


Set Theory & Algebra: UGC NET CSE | December 2009 | Part 2 | Question: 01

Suppose that \( R_1 \) and \( R_2 \) are reflecive relations on a set A. Which of the following statements is correct?



Set Theory & Algebra: UGC NET CSE | July 2016 | Part 2 | Question: 3
options: \( R_1 \cap R_2 \) is reflexive and \( R_1 \cup R_2 \) is irreflexive
\( R_1 \cap R_2 \) is irreflexive and \( R_1 \cup R_2 \) is reflexive
Both \( R_1 \cap R_2 \) and \( R_1 \cup R_2 \) are reflexive
Both \( R_1 \cap R_2 \) and \( R_1 \cup R_2 \) are irreflexive
questions.correct_answer: Both \( R_1 \cap R_2 \) and \( R_1 \cup R_2 \) are reflexive

questions.question:
The transitive closure of a relation \( R \) on set \( A \) whose relation matrix \( \begin{bmatrix}
0 & 1 & 0\\ 
0 & 0 & 1 \\ 
1 & 0 & 0
\end{bmatrix} \) is :

 



Set Theory & Algebra: UGC NET CSE | June 2005 | Part 2 | Question: 3

"\( x^{1} \) is a clone of \( x \)" means \( x^{1} \) is identical to \( x \) in terms of the physical attributes namely, height, weight and complexion. Given, height, weight and complexion only form a complete set of attributes for an entity, cloning is an equivalence relation. What is your impression about this statement ?



Set Theory & Algebra: UGC NET CSE | June 2010 | Part 2 | Question: 1
options: The truth value of the statement cannot be computed

questions.question:
The relation “devides” on a set of positive integers is_____



Set Theory & Algebra: UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 2 | Question: 34
